scriptText:
I. PART 1

[[STAGING]]
[[INTENT]]
Create a cinematic drama continuous shot (oner) capturing the high-pressure chaos of a 1997 family restaurant kitchen. Tone: Gritty, frantic, unpolished.
[[/INTENT]]
[[LOGIC]]
Maintain spatial continuity between the stove line and the service pass. Ensure handheld kinetic energy reflects Mark’s internal stress. Prioritize stable background geometry of the 1920s brick/stainless kitchen.
[[/LOGIC]]
[[AESTHETIC]]
- 1997 Period; 1920s converted storefront; patchwork repairs; cramped/claustrophobic.
- Lighting: High-contrast; warm blue stove flames vs. cold flickering fluorescent plating station.
- @Image 1  Mark: Faded navy coat ("MARK" embroidered lopsided), rolled cuffs, yellowed collar.
- Atmosphere: Heavy steam, grease-filmed air, frantic tempo.
- Audio: Clacking knives, hissing pans, tinny "Bullet with Butterfly Wings" on a grease-coated radio.
- Props: Ceramic pasta plate, parsley, stainless steel pass.
[[/AESTHETIC]]
[[/STAGING]]

[<BRIEF>]
[CAM: HANDHELD REAR-FOLLOW] Close on Mark’s sweat-slicked navy back -> [ACT] He navigates a gauntlet of stainless steel; L: roaring blue Vulcan stove flames; R: cook scrubbing spills -> [AUDIO] Industrial kitchen cacophony + distorted 90s rock.
[ACT] Mark bellows: "Chanterelles in two minutes or I'm locking the back door!" -> [ACT] He lunges to a cramped corner under a flickering bulb. 
[CAM: 90-DEGREE SWING TO PROFILE TRACK] -> [ACT] Mark’s raw, stained hands swirl a pasta nest; steam veils his face; jaw tight, temple vein pulsing -> [ENV] Thick steam billows from pans. 
[ACT] Aggressive parsley flick; Mark grabs the plate, stepping toward the brick service pass.
[CAM: PUSH TO CU] -> [ACT] Mark slams plate on the stainless ledge: "PICK UP! TABLE 4! ROSA, GET OVER HERE!" 
[CAM: CONTINUOUS PUSH THROUGH PASS] -> [ACT] Mark spins back, disappearing into a cloud of sauté steam; camera holds on the steaming pasta plate framed by the pass opening looking into the dim dining room.
[</BRIEF>]

II. PART 2

[[STAGING]]
[[INTENT]]
Create a continuous drama shot from @Video 1  transitioning from chaotic kitchen heat to a hazy, atmospheric 1997 dining room. Tone: Hectic, weary, period-authentic.
[[/INTENT]]
[[LOGIC]]
Ensure a seamless threshold crossing through the service pass window. Prioritize the dramatic shift in soundscape and lighting color temperature (cool kitchen vs. amber dining room). Maintain consistent tracking distance behind Rosa to anchor the viewer in the environment.
[[/LOGIC]]
[[AESTHETIC]]
1997 Dining Room: Red vinyl booths; 1920s tin ceilings; amber string lights; light haze of cigarette smoke.
@Image 1  Rosa: 90s shag haircut; chipped red nail polish; frantic but professional; weary body language.
@Image 2  Robert (Target): Gray polyester shirt; seated in a secluded back booth; staring at the front door.
Props: Metal order-bell; steaming pasta plate; stack of paper napkins.
Sound: Hissing/Alt-rock (BOH) cross-fades into Sinatra’s "Summer Wind," clinking silverware, and muffled chatter (FOH).
[[/AESTHETIC]]
[[/STAGING]]

[<BRIEF>]
[CAM: POV/INS THROUGH PASS] Frame centered on steaming pasta plate on the ledge -> [ACT] Rosa’s hands (chipped red polish) enter; her elbow hits the metal bell (DING-CLATTER); she catches it mid-slide -> [AUDIO] Kitchen roar fades/muffles as "Summer Wind" and dining room chatter rise.
[ACT] Rosa tucks a hair strand; grabs plate/napkins; yells back: "I’m here, Mark! One of 'em is holding up this entire Friday night!" -> [ACT] She pivots, turning her back to the kitchen.
[CAM: MEDIUM REAR-FOLLOW TRACK] Camera "detaches" from the pass, trailing 3ft behind Rosa -> [ACT] She weaves through narrow aisles between red booths; clips a chair corner causing a diner to flinch; continues forward with professional focus -> [ENV] Amber glow and light cigarette smoke haze.
[ACT] Rosa (to passing busser): "Watch the water on six!" -> [ACT] She approaches the back corner booth where Robert (gray polyester shirt) sits staring at the entrance -> [CAM] Movement slows as she nears the table, shifting focus to the waiting man.
[</BRIEF>]

III. PART 3

[[STAGING]]
[[GLOBAL]]
Create a continuous drama shot from @Video 1 in a hazy, atmospheric 1997 dining room. Tone: calm, weary, period-authentic. Ensure the result is cinematic, logical with strong frame and composition.
[[/GLOBAL]]
[[CONTINUITY]]
Continuing from the final frame of @Video 1 , the camera is currently in a REAR-FOLLOW MEDIUM SHOT on @Image 1  ROSA’s back. The logic here is a "momentum hand-off." The energy ROSA carried from Mark in the kitchen is physically placed onto ROBERT’s table. The audio shifts from the "active" sounds of the restaurant (footsteps, wind through the door, distant music) to an "intimate" soundstage—ROBERT’s voice and the tactile sounds of the table become the primary audio layer.
[[/CONTINUITY]]
[[VISUAL BLOCKING]]
The frame begins with ROSA’s silhouette centered as she approaches the booth. As she reaches the table, the camera performs a SLOW ORBIT TO THE LEFT, moving from her back to a 3/4 MEDIUM SHOT that captures both @Image 1  ROSA (profile) and @Image 2  ROBERT (seated). @Image 1  ROSA stands to the right of the table. Her left hand remains high, holding the second plate steady, while her right hand lowers the plate onto the table. @Image 2  ROBERT is seated, framed by the dark red vinyl of the booth. After the interaction, @Image 1  ROSA exits the frame by walking "past" the camera to the left, leaving the frame entirely to @Image 2  ROBERT. He remains centered, a solitary figure in the warm amber glow.
[[/VISUAL BLOCKING]]
[[/STAGING]]

[MEDIUM SHOT – THE BOOTH]
As ROSA reaches the table, the camera’s forward momentum slows and begins to pivot left. We see ROBERT. He looks up, a weary but kind smile forming.
The sound of the restaurant’s jazz fades into a muffled, underwater hum. The sharp clack of the ceramic plate hitting the Formica tabletop is loud and clear.

ROSA:
(Voice dropping to a warm, familiar tone)
The special, ROBERT. Mark made sure the chanterelles were fresh-cut for you.

She keeps the second plate balanced perfectly in her left hand. ROBERT lets out a soft, gravelly chuckle, the sound of a long-time regular.

ROBERT:
Thank you, ROSA. It looks delicious.

As he speaks, ROSA reaches out with her free right hand and gives his shoulder a brief, affectionate pat.

ROSA:
Enjoy, ROBERT.

ROSA pivots and walks directly toward the left of the frame.

[STATIC MEDIUM SHOT – ROBERT]
The camera is now still. ROBERT is alone in the frame. The steam from the pasta rises between him and the lens. He doesn't pick up his fork. Instead, he looks at the empty seat across from him for a beat.
His left hand reaches into his shirt pocket. He pulls out a weathered, physical 4x6 photograph. The camera stays at a distance—we see the white border and the faded color of the 90s print, but the image itself remains a private secret, angled toward him.

ROBERT:
(A ghost of a whisper)
Wish you were here, kid.

The muffled sound of the kitchen bell DINGS one last time in the far distance.

[MEDIUM SHOT – THE BOOTH]
The sound of the restaurant’s jazz fades into a muffled, underwater hum. The sharp clack of the ceramic plate hitting the Formica tabletop is loud and clear.

cues.selectedText: The special, ROBERT. Mark made sure the chanterelles were fresh-cut for you.

As he speaks, ROSA reaches out with her free right hand and gives his shoulder a brief, affectionate pat.
ROBERT lets out a soft, gravelly chuckle, the sound of a long-time regular.

cues.speaker: Rosa
cues.selectedText: Enjoy, ROBERT.
ROSA pivots and walks directly toward the left of the frame.
[STATIC MEDIUM SHOT – ROBERT]

The camera is now still. ROBERT is alone in the frame. The steam from the pasta rises between him and the lens. He doesn't pick up his fork. Instead, he looks at the empty seat across from him for a beat.
His left hand reaches into his shirt pocket. He pulls out a weathered, physical 4x6 photograph.
The camera stays at a distance—we see the white border and the faded color of the 90s print, but the image itself remains a private secret, angled toward him.

cues.speaker: Robert
